Common Cold.advice: Rest at home, stay hydrated, and take over-the-counter medicine if needed.
Common Cold.description: A viral infection of your nose and throat (upper respiratory tract).

Flu.advice: Rest and monitor symptoms. Consult a doctor if fever persists or breathing becomes difficult.
Flu.description: A common viral infection that can be deadly, especially in high-risk groups.

COVID-19.advice: Isolate yourself and consult a medical professional immediately. Monitor oxygen levels.
COVID-19.description: An infectious disease caused by the SARS-CoV-2 virus.

Migraine.advice: Rest in a dark, quiet room. If pain is severe or unusual, consult a doctor.
Migraine.description: A headache that can cause severe throbbing pain or a pulsing sensation, usually on one side of the head.

Pneumonia.advice: Requires medical evaluation. See a doctor soon as it can become severe.
Pneumonia.description: An infection that inflames the air sacs in one or both lungs.

Malaria.advice: Seek immediate medical attention. Requires specific anti-malarial treatment.
Malaria.description: A disease caused by a parasite, transmitted through the bite of infected mosquitoes.

Typhoid.advice: Consult a doctor for antibiotics. Untreated typhoid can be serious.
Typhoid.description: A bacterial infection that can spread throughout the body, affecting many organs.

Gastroenteritis.advice: Focus on rehydration. See a doctor if you can't keep fluids down or have severe pain.
Gastroenteritis.description: An intestinal infection marked by diarrhea, cramps, nausea, vomiting, and fever.

Allergy.advice: Avoid triggers. Antihistamines may help. Consult an allergist for persistent issues.
Allergy.description: A condition in which the immune system reacts abnormally to a foreign substance.

Asthma.advice: Use your rescue inhaler. Seek emergency care if breathing doesn't improve.
Asthma.description: A condition in which your airways narrow and swell and may produce extra mucus.
